## Patient Dosing Summary

| Patient ID | Age (months) | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated Dose (mg) | Frequency | Next Dose | PK Clearance (mL/min) | Notes |

## Quick Assessment

- All three compassionate-use patients have defined dosing parameters in the latest v3 config.
- PK clearance values are present for each patient.
- Based on the dosing file alone, there are no obvious configuration gaps or inconsistencies.
- Clinical notes in the latest config suggest CU-001 and CU-002 remain clinically stable/responding; CU-003 is newly enrolled.

## Caveat

This assessment reflects the latest dosing configuration only. It does **not** confirm live PK model runtime health or recent monitoring status beyond what is captured in the config file.", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and prepared a summary for Dr. Martinez.

Quick summary from `helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`:
- Compound: NXB-7042

Patient dosing:
- CU-001: 12.4 kg × 0.15 mg/kg = 1.86 mg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-14, PK clearance 3.2 mL/min
- CU-002: 9.1 kg × 0.12 mg/kg = 1.09 mg, q3w, next dose 2026-04-12, PK clearance 2.8 mL/min
- CU-003: 15.7 kg × 0.18 mg/kg = 2.83 mg, q2w, next dose 2026-04-10, PK clearance 4.1 mL/min

Clinical notes:
- CU-001: Stable, tumor regression 22% at last scan
- CU-002: Responding well, weight gain improving
- CU-003: New enrollment, first dose given 2026-03-27

On PK stability: from the dosing config alone, everything looks populated and internally consistent, and there are no obvious red flags in the patient notes. That said, this does not confirm live PK model runtime health or recent monitoring status — only that the latest dosing parameters themselves look in order.

If you want, I can also format this into an email once you give me Dr. Martinez’s address.
